description: The point or position equally distant from extremes; the center. 'Kesho' names the balanced point—neither beginning nor end, the place of equipoise.
sound_symbolism: The word balances. 'Ke' marks with clarity, position defined. Then 'sho' /ʃo/ settles at center, middle held.
grammatical_notes: Functions as a noun: 'lo kesho' (the middle). Between 'thorui' (beginning) and 'lumae' (end). Also relates to the Middle Way in Buddhist philosophy.

pillars.secular-buddhist-philosophy: The Middle Way avoids extremes—the balanced path between indulgence and denial, grasping and aversion.
pillars.pre-industrial-wisdom: Middle positions held power—the middle child, the mid-season, the balance point where leverage works.
